issues_found: event_type 'monetary_policy_report' is questionable - source is an educational/explainer page describing the inflation target framework, not a substantive policy report
Source is thin on archival value - it explains the existing framework rather than providing new analysis, decisions, or research
Governor letters to the Chancellor (Dec 2025, Sep 2025, Jun 2025) are referenced but not included in this page - the actual substantive policy communications are absent
Published_at derived from 'last updated' (18 Feb 2026) rather than an original publication date; this page has existed much longer
Record describes this page as establishing 'foundational monetary policy framework' which overstates its macro catalyst relevance
human_review_reason: This record is borderline. The factual content is accurate (Bank Rate 3.75%, inflation 3%, 2% target, measurement methodology), but the source is an institutional explainer page rather than substantive macro catalyst content. The event_type classification as 'monetary_policy_report' is questionable - this is more akin to institutional background than a policy report. Given the archive's focus on market structure and macro catalysts, this thin educational content may not warrant archiving when the actual Governor letters are the substantive communications.

corrected_fields.why_it_matters: The why_it_matters reasonably interprets the Governor letter requirement but overstates the page's significance - this explainer page references those letters rather than containing them.
